title: Domain Records, Pure Pipeline Stages, and Serialization · Python Build Room
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds typed cumulative analysis pipeline from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed cumulative analysis pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
Use immutable records where post-computation mutation would invalidate retained evidence.
Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Domain Records
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 6563 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose typed stages and immutable evidence under a versioned serialization schema; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-serialization-schema-19okg4
# Competency: bookbot-serialization-schema.
# Case 3298: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_serialization_schema_19okg4(stages: int = 7, immutable_records: bool = True, typed_boundaries: bool = True, schema_version: int = 1, stage_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_19okg4 = "19okg4-4gko91-3298"
    assert evidence_variant_19okg4
    evidence_axes_19okg4 = (
        "corpus19okg4",
        "policy4gko91",
        "revision3298",
        "command19okg43298",
        "report4gko913298",
        "test329819okg4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_19okg4)) == 6
    if stages < 5 or schema_version < 1:
        return False, stages, "pipeline-shape"
    if not immutable_records:
        return False, stages, "mutable-evidence"
    if not typed_boundaries:
        return False, stages, "untyped-stage"
    if stage_failures > 0:
        return False, stage_failures, "stage-failure"
    assert stages >= 5
    return True, schema_version, "versioned-pipeline"
